units.text: Why, he’s a devil, a devil, a very fiend.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Why, she’s a devil, a devil, the devil’s
dam.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text:
Tut, she’s a lamb, a dove, a fool to him!
I’ll tell you, Sir Lucentio: when the priest
Should ask if Katherine should be his wife,
“Ay, by gogs wouns!” quoth he, and swore so loud
That, all amazed, the priest let fall the book,
And as he stooped again to take it up,
The mad-brained bridegroom took him such a cuff
That down fell priest and book, and book and priest.
“Now take them up,” quoth he, “if any list.”

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: [as LUCENTIO] What said the wench when he rose again?

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text:
Trembled and shook, for why he stamped and swore
As if the vicar meant to cozen him.
But after many ceremonies done,
He calls for wine. “A health!” quoth he, as if
He had been aboard, carousing to his mates
After a storm; quaffed off the muscatel
And threw the sops all in the sexton’s face,
Having no other reason
But that his beard grew thin and hungerly
And seemed to ask him sops as he was drinking.
This done, he took the bride about the neck
And kissed her lips with such a clamorous smack
That at the parting all the church did echo.
And I, seeing this, came thence for very shame,
And after me, I know, the rout is coming.
Such a mad marriage never was before.
Music

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Hark, hark! I hear the minstrels play.
Enter PETRUCHIO, KATHERINE, BIANCA, BAPTISTA, HORTENSIO, GRUMIO, and train

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Gentlemen and friends, I thank you for you
I know you think to dine with me today
And have prepared great store of wedding cheer,
But so it is, my haste doth call me hence,
And therefore here I mean to take my leave.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Is ’t possible you will away tonight?

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
I must away today, before night come.
Make it no wonder. If you knew my business,
You would entreat me rather go than stay.
And, honest company, I thank you all,
That have beheld me give away myself
To this most patient, sweet and virtuous wife.
Dine with my father, drink a health to me,
For I must hence, and farewell to you all.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Let us entreat you stay till after
dinner.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: It may not be.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Let me entreat you.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: It cannot be.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Let me entreat you.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: I am content.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Are you content to stay?

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
I am content you shall entreat me stay,
But yet not stay, entreat me how you can.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Now, if you love me, stay.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Grumio, my horse.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Ay, sir, they be ready. The oats have eaten the horses.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Nay, then,
Do what thou canst, I will not go today,
No, nor tomorrow, not till I please myself.
The door is open, sir. There lies your way.
You may be jogging whiles your boots are green.
For me, I’ll not be gone till I please myself.
'Tis like you’ll prove a jolly surly groom,
That take it on you at the first so roundly.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: O Kate, content thee. Prithee, be not angry.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
I will be angry. What hast thou to do?—
Father, be quiet. He shall stay my leisure.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Ay, marry, sir, now it begins to work.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Gentlemen, forward to the bridal dinner.
I see a woman may be made a fool
If she had not a spirit to resist.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
They shall go forward, Kate, at thy command.—
Obey the bride, you that attend on her.
Go to the feast, revel and domineer,
Carouse full measure to her maidenhead,
Be mad and merry, or go hang yourselves.
But for my bonny Kate, she must with me.
Nay, look not big, nor stamp, nor stare, nor fret;
I will be master of what is mine own.
She is my goods, my chattels; she is my house,
My household stuff, my field, my barn,
My horse, my ox, my ass, my anything.
And here she stands, touch her whoever dare.
I’ll bring mine action on the proudest he
That stops my way in Padua. —Grumio,
Draw forth thy weapon, we are beset with thieves.
Rescue thy mistress if thou be a man.—
Fear not, sweet wench, they shall not touch thee, Kate.
I’ll buckler thee against a million.
Exeunt PETRUCHIO, KATHERINE, and GRUMIO

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Nay, let them go, a couple of quiet ones.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Went they not quickly, I should die with laughing.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: [as LUCENTIO] Of all mad matches never was the like.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
[as CAMBIO] Mistress, what’s your opinion of your
sister?

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: That, being mad herself, she’s madly mated.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: I warrant him, Petruchio is Kated.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text:
Neighbors and friends, though bride and bridegroom
wants
For to supply the places at the table,
You know there wants no junkets at the feast.
[To TRANIO]
Lucentio, you shall supply the bridegroom’s place,
And let Bianca take her sister’s room.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Shall sweet Bianca practice how to bride
it?

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: She shall, Lucentio. Come, gentlemen, let’s go.
Exeunt
Enter GRUMIO

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Fie, fie on all tired jades, on all mad masters, and
all foul ways! Was ever man so beaten? Was ever man so
'rayed? Was ever man so weary? I am sent before to make
a fire, and they are coming after to warm them. Now,
were not I a little pot and soon hot, my very lips might
freeze to my teeth, my tongue to the roof of my mouth,
my heart in my belly, ere I should come by a fire to
thaw me. But I with blowing the fire shall warm myself.
For, considering the weather, a taller man than I will
take cold.—Holla, ho! Curtis!
Enter CURTIS

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Who is that calls so coldly?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
A piece of ice. If thou doubt it, thou mayst slide from
my shoulder to my heel with no greater a run but my
head and my neck. A fire, good Curtis.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Is my master and his wife coming, Grumio?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
O, ay, Curtis, ay, and therefore fire, fire. Cast on no
water.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Is she so hot a shrew as she’s reported?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
She was, good Curtis, before this frost. But thou
knowest winter tames man, woman and beast, for it hath
tamed my old master and my new mistress and myself,
fellow Curtis.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Away, you three-inch fool! I am no beast.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Am I but three inches? Why, thy horn is a foot, and so
long am I, at the least. But wilt thou make a fire, or
shall I complain on thee to our mistress, whose hand,
she being now at hand, thou shalt soon feel, to thy cold
comfort, for being slow in thy hot office?

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: I prithee, good Grumio, tell me, how goes the world?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
A cold world, Curtis, in every office but thine, and
therefore fire! Do thy duty, and have thy duty, for my
master and mistress are almost frozen to death.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text:
There’s fire ready. And therefore, good Grumio, the
news.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Why, “Jack, boy! Ho, boy!” and as much news as wilt
thou.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Come, you are so full of cony-catching!

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Why, therefore fire, for I have caught extreme cold.
Where’s the cook? Is supper ready, the house trimmed,
rushes strewed, cobwebs swept, the servingmen in their
new fustian, their white stockings, and every officer
his wedding garment on? Be the Jacks fair within, the
Jills fair without, the carpets laid, and everything in
order?

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: All ready. And therefore, I pray thee, news.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
First, know my horse is tired, my master and mistress
fallen out.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: How?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Out of their saddles into the dirt, and thereby hangs a
tale.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Let’s ha' ’t, good Grumio.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Lend thine ear.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Here.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: There!

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: This ’tis to feel a tale, not to hear a tale.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
And therefore ’tis called a sensible tale. And this
cuff was but to knock at your ear and beseech list'ning.
Now I begin: Imprimis, we came down a foul hill, my
master riding behind my mistress—

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Both of one horse?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: What’s that to thee?

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Why, a horse.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Tell thou the tale! But hadst thou not crossed me, thou
shouldst have heard how her horse fell, and she under
her horse. Thou shouldst have heard in how miry a place,
how she was bemoiled, how he left her with the horse
upon her, how he beat me because her horse stumbled, how
she waded through the dirt to pluck him off me, how he
swore, how she prayed that never prayed before, how I
cried, how the horses ran away, how her bridle was
burst, how I lost my crupper, with many things of worthy
memory which now shall die in oblivion, and thou return
unexperienced to thy grave.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: By this reck'ning he is more shrew than she.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Ay, and that thou and the proudest of you all shall
find when he comes home. But what talk I of this? Call
forth Nathaniel, Joseph, Nicholas, Philip, Walter,
Sugarsop, and the rest. Let their heads be slickly
combed, their blue coats brushed, and their garters of
an indifferent knit. Let them curtsy with their left
legs, and not presume to touch a hair of my master’s
horse-tail till they kiss their hands. Are they all
ready?

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: They are.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Call them forth.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text:
[calling offstage] Do you hear, ho? you must meet my
master to countenance my mistress.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Why, she hath a face of her own.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: Who knows not that?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Thou, it seems, that calls for company to countenance
her.

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text: I call them forth to credit her.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Why, she comes to borrow nothing of them.
Enter four or five Servingmen

units.speaker: NATHANIEL
units.text: Welcome home, Grumio.

units.speaker: PHILIP
units.text: How now, Grumio?

units.speaker: JOSEPH
units.text: What, Grumio!

units.speaker: NICHOLAS
units.text: Fellow Grumio!

units.speaker: NATHANIEL
units.text: How now, old lad?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Welcome, you!—How now, you?—What, you!—Fellow,
you!—And thus much for greeting. Now, my spruce
companions, is all ready, and all things neat?

units.speaker: NATHANIEL
units.text: All things is ready. How near is our master?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
E'en at hand, alighted by this. And therefore be not—
Cock’s passion, silence! I hear my master.
Enter PETRUCHIO and KATHERINE

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Where be these knaves? What, no man at door
To hold my stirrup nor to take my horse!
Where is Nathaniel, Gregory, Philip?

units.speaker: ALL SERVINGMEN
units.text: Here, here, sir! Here, sir!

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
“Here, sir! Here, sir! Here, sir! Here, sir!”
You loggerheaded and unpolished grooms!
What, no attendance? No regard? No duty?
Where is the foolish knave I sent before?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Here, sir, as foolish as I was before.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
You peasant swain! You whoreson malt-horse drudge!
Did I not bid thee meet me in the park
And bring along these rascal knaves with thee?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Nathaniel’s coat, sir, was not fully made,
And Gabriel’s pumps were all unpinked i' th' heel.
There was no link to color Peter’s hat,
And Walter’s dagger was not come from sheathing.
There were none fine but Adam, Rafe, and Gregory.
The rest were ragged, old, and beggarly.
Yet, as they are, here are they come to meet you.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Go, rascals, go, and fetch my supper in.
Exeunt Servants

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Singing
Where is the life that late I led—
Where are those —Sit down, Kate, and welcome.—
Soud, soud, soud, soud!
Enter Servants with supper

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Why, when, I say?—Nay, good sweet Kate, be merry.—
Off with my boots, you rogues! You villains, when?
Exit a servant

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
One, Kate, that you must kiss and be acquainted with.—
Where are my slippers? Shall I have some water?—
Enter one with water

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Come, Kate, and wash, and welcome heartily.—
You whoreson villain! Will you let it fall?
Strikes him
[He strikes the servant]

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Patience, I pray you! 'Twas a fault unwilling.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
A whoreson, beetle-headed, flap-eared knave!—
Come, Kate, sit down. I know you have a stomach.
Will you give thanks, sweet Kate, or else shall I?—
What’s this? Mutton?

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text: Ay.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Who brought it?

units.speaker: PETER
units.text: I.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
'Tis burnt, and so is all the meat.
What dogs are these! Where is the rascal cook?
How durst you, villains, bring it from the dresser
And serve it thus to me that love it not?
There, take it to you, trenchers, cups, and all!
Throws the meat, & c. about the stage
You heedless joltheads and unmannered slaves!
What, do you grumble? I’ll be with you straight.
Exeunt servants

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
I pray you, husband, be not so disquiet.
The meat was well, if you were so contented.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
I tell thee, Kate, ’twas burnt and dried away.
And I expressly am forbid to touch it,
For it engenders choler, planteth anger;
And better ’twere that both of us did fast,
Since of ourselves, ourselves are choleric,
Than feed it with such over-roasted flesh.
Be patient, tomorrow ’t shall be mended,
And, for this night, we’ll fast for company.
Come, I will bring thee to thy bridal chamber.
Exeunt
Enter Servants severally

units.speaker: NATHANIEL
units.text: Peter, didst ever see the like?

units.speaker: PETER
units.text: He kills her in her own humor.
Enter CURTIS

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Where is he?

units.speaker: CURTIS
units.text:
In her chamber,
Making a sermon of continency to her,
And rails and swears and rates, that she, poor soul,
Knows not which way to stand, to look, to speak,
And sits as one new-risen from a dream.
Away, away, for he is coming hither!
Exeunt
Enter PETRUCHIO

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Thus have I politicly begun my reign,
And ’tis my hope to end successfully.
My falcon now is sharp and passing empty,
And, till she stoop, she must not be full-gorged,
For then she never looks upon her lure.
Another way I have to man my haggard,
To make her come and know her keeper’s call.
That is, to watch her, as we watch these kites
That bate and beat and will not be obedient.
She ate no meat today, nor none shall eat.
Last night she slept not, nor tonight she shall not.
As with the meat, some undeservèd fault
I’ll find about the making of the bed,
And here I’ll fling the pillow, there the bolster,
This way the coverlet, another way the sheets.
Ay, and amid this hurly I intend
That all is done in reverend care of her.
And, in conclusion, she shall watch all night,
And if she chance to nod I’ll rail and brawl,
And with the clamor keep her still awake.
This is a way to kill a wife with kindness,
And thus I’ll curb her mad and headstrong humor.
He that knows better how to tame a shrew,
Now let him speak; ’tis charity to show.
Exit
Enter TRANIO as LUCENTIO and HORTENSIO as LITIO

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO]
Is ’t possible, friend Litio, that mistress Bianca
Doth fancy any other but Lucentio?
I tell you, sir, she bears me fair in hand.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
[as LITIO] Sir, to satisfy you in what I have said,
Stand by and mark the manner of his teaching.
They stand aside
Enter BIANCA and LUCENTIO as CAMBIO

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: [as CAMBIO] Now, mistress, profit you in what you read?

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: What, master, read you? First resolve me that.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: [as CAMBIO] I read that I profess, The Art to Love.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: And may you prove, sir, master of your art.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
[as CAMBIO] While you, sweet dear, prove mistress of
my heart!

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
[as LITIO] Quick proceeders, marry! Now, tell me, I
pray,
You that durst swear that your mistress Bianca
Loved none in the world so well as Lucentio.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] O despiteful love! Unconstant womankind!
I tell thee, Litio, this is wonderful!

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
Mistake no more. I am not Litio,
Nor a musician as I seem to be,
But one that scorn to live in this disguise
For such a one as leaves a gentleman
And makes a god of such a cullion.
Know, sir, that I am called Hortensio.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Signior Hortensio, I have often heard
Of your entire affection to Bianca,
And since mine eyes are witness of her lightness,
I will with you, if you be so contented,
Forswear Bianca and her love for ever.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
See how they kiss and court! Signior Lucentio,
Here is my hand, and here I firmly vow
Never to woo her more, but do forswear her
As one unworthy all the former favors
That I have fondly flattered her withal.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
And here I take the like unfeignèd oath
Never to marry with her, though she would entreat.
Fie on her! See how beastly she doth court him!

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
Would all the world but he had quite forsworn!
For me, that I may surely keep mine oath,
I will be married to a wealthy widow,
Ere three days pass, which hath as long loved me
As I have loved this proud disdainful haggard.
And so farewell, Signior Lucentio.
Kindness in women, not their beauteous looks,
Shall win my love, and so I take my leave,
In resolution as I swore before.
Exit

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
Mistress Bianca, bless you with such grace
As 'longeth to a lover’s blessèd case!
Nay, I have ta'en you napping, gentle love,
And have forsworn you with Hortensio.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Tranio, you jest. But have you both forsworn me?

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: Mistress, we have.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: Then we are rid of Litio.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
I' faith, he’ll have a lusty widow now
That shall be wooed and wedded in a day.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: God give him joy!

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: Ay, and he’ll tame her.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: He says so, Tranio?

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: Faith, he is gone unto the taming school.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: The taming school? What, is there such a place?

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
Ay, mistress, and Petruchio is the master,
That teacheth tricks eleven and twenty long
To tame a shrew and charm her chattering tongue.
Enter BIONDELLO

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
O master, master, I have watched so long
That I am dog-weary, but at last I spied
An ancient angel coming down the hill
Will serve the turn.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: What is he, Biondello?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
Master, a marcantant, or a pedant,
I know not what, but formal in apparel,
In gait and countenance surely like a father.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: And what of him, Tranio?

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
If he be credulous and trust my tale,
I’ll make him glad to seem Vincentio
And give assurance to Baptista Minola
As if he were the right Vincentio.
Take in your love, and then let me alone.
Exeunt LUCENTIO and BIANCA
Enter a MERCHANT

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text: God save you, sir.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO]
And you, sir. You are welcome.
Travel you far on, or are you at the farthest?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
Sir, at the farthest for a week or two,
But then up farther, and as far as Rome,
And so to Tripoli, if God lend me life.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: [as LUCENTIO] What countryman, I pray?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text: Of Mantua.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Of Mantua, sir? Marry, God forbid!
And come to Padua, careless of your life?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text: My life, sir! how, I pray? For that goes hard.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
'Tis death for anyone in Mantua
To come to Padua. Know you not the cause?
Your ships are stayed at Venice, and the Duke,
For private quarrel ’twixt your duke and him,
Hath published and proclaimed it openly.
'Tis marvel, but that you are but newly come,
You might have heard it else proclaimed about.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
Alas, sir, it is worse for me than so,
For I have bills for money by exchange
From Florence, and must here deliver them.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Well, sir, to do you courtesy,
This will I do, and this I will advise you.
First tell me, have you ever been at Pisa?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
Ay, sir, in Pisa have I often been,
Pisa renownèd for grave citizens.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: [as LUCENTIO] Among them know you one Vincentio?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
I know him not, but I have heard of him:
A merchant of incomparable wealth.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] He is my father, sir, and sooth to say,
In count'nance somewhat doth resemble you.

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
[aside] As much as an apple doth an oyster, and all
one.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] To save your life in this extremity,
This favor will I do you for his sake—
And think it not the worst of all your fortunes
That you are like to Sir Vincentio—
His name and credit shall you undertake,
And in my house you shall be friendly lodged.
Look that you take upon you as you should.
You understand me, sir. So shall you stay
Till you have done your business in the city.
If this be court’sy, sir, accept of it.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
O sir, I do, and will repute you ever
The patron of my life and liberty.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
(as LUCENTIO ) Then go with me to make the matter good.
This, by the way, I let you understand:
My father is here looked for every day
To pass assurance of a dower in marriage
'Twixt me and one Baptista’s daughter here.
In all these circumstances I’ll instruct you.
Go with me to clothe you as becomes you.
Exeunt
Enter KATHERINE and GRUMIO

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: No, no, forsooth, I dare not for my life.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
The more my wrong, the more his spite appears.
What, did he marry me to famish me?
Beggars that come unto my father’s door
Upon entreaty have a present alms.
If not, elsewhere they meet with charity.
But I, who never knew how to entreat,
Nor never needed that I should entreat,
Am starved for meat, giddy for lack of sleep,
With oaths kept waking and with brawling fed.
And that which spites me more than all these wants,
He does it under name of perfect love,
As who should say, if I should sleep or eat,
'Twere deadly sickness or else present death.
I prithee, go and get me some repast,
I care not what, so it be wholesome food.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: What say you to a neat’s foot?

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: 'Tis passing good. I prithee let me have it.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
I fear it is too choleric a meat.
How say you to a fat tripe finely broiled?

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: I like it well. Good Grumio, fetch it me.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
I cannot tell. I fear ’tis choleric. What say you to a
piece of beef and mustard?

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: A dish that I do love to feed upon.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Ay, but the mustard is too hot a little.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Why then, the beef, and let the mustard rest.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Nay then, I will not. You shall have the mustard
Or else you get no beef of Grumio.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Then both, or one, or anything thou wilt.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Why then, the mustard without the beef.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Go, get thee gone, thou false deluding slave,
(Beats him)
That feed’st me with the very name of meat.
Sorrow on thee and all the pack of you
That triumph thus upon my misery.
Go, get thee gone, I say.
Enter PETRUCHIO and HORTENSIO with meat

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: How fares my Kate? What, sweeting, all amort?

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: Mistress, what cheer?

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Faith, as cold as can be.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Pluck up thy spirits. Look cheerfully upon me.
Here love, thou seest how diligent I am,
To dress thy meat myself and bring it thee.
I am sure, sweet Kate, this kindness merits thanks.
What, not a word? Nay, then thou lov’st it not
And all my pains is sorted to no proof.
Here, take away this dish.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: I pray you, let it stand.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
The poorest service is repaid with thanks,
And so shall mine, before you touch the meat.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: I thank you, sir.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
Signior Petruchio, fie, you are to blame.
Come, mistress Kate, I’ll bear you company.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
[aside to HORTENSIO ]
Eat it up all, Hortensio, if thou lovest me.—
Much good do it unto thy gentle heart.
Kate, eat apace. And now, my honey love,
Will we return unto thy father’s house
And revel it as bravely as the best,
With silken coats and caps and golden rings,
With ruffs and cuffs and farthingales and things,
With scarves and fans and double change of brav'ry,
With amber bracelets, beads, and all this knav'ry.
What, hast thou dined? The tailor stays thy leisure
To deck thy body with his ruffling treasure.
Enter TAILOR

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Come, tailor, let us see these ornaments.
Lay forth the gown.
Enter HABERDASHER

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: What news with you, sir?

units.speaker: HABERDASHER
units.text: Here is the cap your Worship did bespeak.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Why, this was molded on a porringer!
A velvet dish! Fie, fie, ’tis lewd and filthy!
Why, ’tis a cockle or a walnut shell,
A knack, a toy, a trick, a baby’s cap.
Away with it! Come, let me have a bigger.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
I’ll have no bigger. This doth fit the time,
And gentlewomen wear such caps as these.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
When you are gentle, you shall have one too,
And not till then.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: [aside] That will not be in haste.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Why, sir, I trust I may have leave to speak,
And speak I will. I am no child, no babe.
Your betters have endured me say my mind,
And if you cannot, best you stop your ears.
My tongue will tell the anger of my heart
Or else my heart, concealing it, will break,
And, rather than it shall, I will be free
Even to the uttermost, as I please, in words.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Why, thou say’st true. It is a paltry cap,
A custard-coffin, a bauble, a silken pie.
I love thee well in that thou lik’st it not.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Love me or love me not, I like the cap,
And it I will have, or I will have none.
Exit HABERDASHER

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Thy gown? Why, ay. Come, tailor, let us see ’t.
O mercy, God! What masking stuff is here?
What’s this? A sleeve? 'Tis like a demi-cannon.
What, up and down, carved like an apple tart?
Here’s snip and nip and cut and slish and slash,
Like to a censer in a barber’s shop.
Why, what i' devil’s name, tailor, call’st thou this?

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: [aside] I see she’s like to have neither cap nor gown.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text:
You bid me make it orderly and well,
According to the fashion and the time.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Marry, and did. But if you be remembered,
I did not bid you mar it to the time.
Go, hop me over every kennel home,
For you shall hop without my custom, sir.
I’ll none of it. Hence, make your best of it.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
I never saw a better-fashioned gown,
More quaint, more pleasing, nor more commendable.
Belike you mean to make a puppet of me.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Why, true, he means to make a puppet of thee.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: She says your Worship means to make a puppet of her.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
O monstrous arrogance! Thou liest, thou thread, thou
thimble,
Thou yard, three-quarters, half-yard, quarter, nail!
Thou flea, thou nit, thou winter cricket thou!
Braved in mine own house with a skein of thread?
Away, thou rag, thou quantity, thou remnant,
Or I shall so be-mete thee with thy yard
As thou shalt think on prating whilst thou liv’st!
I tell thee, I, that thou hast marred her gown.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text:
Your Worship is deceived. The gown is made
Just as my master had direction.
Grumio gave order how it should be done.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: I gave him no order. I gave him the stuff.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: But how did you desire it should be made?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Marry, sir, with needle and thread.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: But did you not request to have it cut?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: Thou hast faced many things.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: I have.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Face not me. Thou hast braved many men; brave not me. I
will neither be faced nor braved. I say unto thee, I
bid thy master cut out the gown, but I did not bid him
cut it to pieces. Ergo, thou liest.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: Why, here is the note of the fashion to testify.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Read it.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: The note lies in ’s throat, if he say I said so.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: [reads] “ Imprimis, a loose-bodied gown—”

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Master, if ever I said “loose-bodied gown,” sew me in
the skirts of it, and beat me to death with a bottom of
brown thread. I said “a gown.”

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Proceed.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: [reads] “With a small-compassed cape—”

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: I confess the cape.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: [reads] “With a trunk sleeve—”

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: I confess two sleeves.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text: [reads] “The sleeves curiously cut.”

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Ay, there’s the villany.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Error i' the bill, sir, error i' the bill! I commanded
the sleeves should be cut out and sewed up again, and
that I’ll prove upon thee, though thy little finger be
armed in a thimble.

units.speaker: TAILOR
units.text:
This is true that I say: an I had thee in place where,
thou shouldst know it.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
I am for thee straight. Take thou the bill, give me thy
mete- yard, and spare not me.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: God-a-mercy, Grumio! Then he shall have no odds.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Well, sir, in brief, the gown is not for me.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text: You are i' the right, sir, ’tis for my mistress.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Go, take it up unto thy master’s use.

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
Villain, not for thy life! Take up my mistress' gown
for thy master’s use!

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Why, sir, what’s your conceit in that?

units.speaker: GRUMIO
units.text:
O, sir, the conceit is deeper than you think for. Take
up my mistress' gown to his master’s use! O, fie, fie,
fie!

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
[aside] Hortensio, say thou wilt see the tailor paid.
[To TAILOR] Go, take it hence. Begone, and say no more.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
[aside to TAILOR ] Tailor, I’ll pay thee for thy gown
tomorrow.
Take no unkindness of his hasty words.
Away, I say. Commend me to thy master.
Exit TAILOR

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Well, come, my Kate. We will unto your father’s
Even in these honest mean habiliments.
Our purses shall be proud, our garments poor,
For ’tis the mind that makes the body rich,
And as the sun breaks through the darkest clouds,
So honor peereth in the meanest habit.
What, is the jay more precious than the lark
Because his feathers are more beautiful?
Or is the adder better than the eel
Because his painted skin contents the eye?
Oh, no, good Kate. Neither art thou the worse
For this poor furniture and mean array.
If thou account’st it shame, lay it on me,
And therefore frolic! We will hence forthwith
To feast and sport us at thy father’s house.
[To GRUMIO ] Go, call my men, and let us straight to
him,
And bring our horses unto Long Lane end.
There will we mount, and thither walk on foot.
Let’s see, I think ’tis now some seven o'clock,
And well we may come there by dinnertime.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
I dare assure you, sir, ’tis almost two,
And ’twill be supper time ere you come there.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
It shall be seven ere I go to horse.
Look what I speak, or do, or think to do,
You are still crossing it. Sirs, let ’t alone.
I will not go today, and ere I do
It shall be what o'clock I say it is.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: [aside] Why, so this gallant will command the sun.
Exeunt
Enter TRANIO as LUCENTIOMERCHANT booted and dressed like VINCENTIO

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Sir, this is the house. Please it you
that I call?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
Ay, what else? and but I be deceived,
Signior Baptista may remember me,
Near twenty years ago, in Genoa,
Where we were lodgers at the Pegasus.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] 'Tis well; and hold your own, in any
case,
With such austerity as 'longeth to a father.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text: I warrant you.
Enter BIONDELLO

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
But, sir, here comes your boy.
'Twere good he were schooled.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Fear you not him.—Sirrah Biondello,
Now do your duty throughly, I advise you.
Imagine ’twere the right Vincentio.

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: Tut, fear not me.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] But hast thou done thy errand to
Baptista?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
I told him that your father was at Venice,
And that you looked for him this day in Padua.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Thou'rt a tall fellow. Hold thee that to
drink.
Gives money
Enter BAPTISTA and LUCENTIO

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: Here comes Baptista. Set your countenance, sir.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
[as VINCENTIO] Soft son.—
Sir, by your leave, having come to Padua
To gather in some debts, my son Lucentio
Made me acquainted with a weighty cause
Of love between your daughter and himself.
And, for the good report I hear of you
And for the love he beareth to your daughter
And she to him, to stay him not too long,
I am content, in a good father’s care,
To have him matched. And if you please to like
No worse than I, upon some agreement
Me shall you find ready and willing
With one consent to have her so bestowed,
For curious I cannot be with you,
Signior Baptista, of whom I hear so well.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text:
Sir, pardon me in what I have to say.
Your plainness and your shortness please me well.
Right true it is your son Lucentio here
Doth love my daughter and she loveth him,
Or both dissemble deeply their affections.
And therefore, if you say no more than this,
That like a father you will deal with him
And pass my daughter a sufficient dower,
The match is made, and all is done.
Your son shall have my daughter with consent.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO]
I thank you, sir. Where then do you know best
We be affied and such assurance ta'en
As shall with either part’s agreement stand?

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text:
Not in my house, Lucentio, for you know
Pitchers have ears, and I have many servants.
Besides, old Gremio is heark'ning still,
And happily we might be interrupted.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Then at my lodging, an it like you.
There doth my father lie, and there this night
We’ll pass the business privately and well.
Send for your daughter by your servant here.
My boy shall fetch the scrivener presently.
The worst is this, that at so slender warning
You are like to have a thin and slender pittance.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text:
It likes me well. —Cambio, hie you home,
And bid Bianca make her ready straight.
And, if you will, tell what hath happenèd:
Lucentio’s father is arrived in Padua,
And how she’s like to be Lucentio’s wife.
Exit LUCENTIO

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: I pray the gods she may, with all my heart!

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Dally not with the gods, but get thee
gone.—
Signior Baptista, shall I lead the way?
Welcome! One mess is like to be your cheer.
Come, sir, we will better it in Pisa.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: I follow you.
Exeunt TRANIO, MERCHANT, and BAPTISTA

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: Cambio.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: What sayest thou, Biondello?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: You saw my master wink and laugh upon you?

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: Biondello, what of that?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
Faith, nothing; but 'has left me here behind to
expound the meaning or moral of his signs and tokens.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: I pray thee, moralize them.

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
Then thus: Baptista is safe, talking with the deceiving
father of a deceitful son.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: And what of him?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: His daughter is to be brought by you to the supper.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: And then?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
The old priest at Saint Luke’s Church is at your
command at all hours.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: And what of all this?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
I cannot tell, except they are busied about a
counterfeit assurance. Take you assurance of her cum
privilegio ad imprimendum solum. To th' church take the
priest, clerk, and some sufficient honest witnesses.
If this be not that you look for, I have no more to
say,
But bid Bianca farewell forever and a day.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: Hear’st thou, Biondello?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
I cannot tarry. I knew a wench married in an afternoon
as she went to the garden for parsley to stuff a rabbit,
and so may you, sir. And so adieu, sir. My master hath
appointed me to go to Saint Luke’s to bid the priest be
ready to come against you come with your appendix.
Exit

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
I may, and will, if she be so contented.
She will be pleased. Then wherefore should I doubt?
Hap what hap may, I’ll roundly go about her.
It shall go hard if “Cambio” go without her.
Exit
Enter PETRUCHIO, KATHERINE, HORTENSIO and Servants

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Come on, i' God’s name, once more toward our father’s.
Good Lord, how bright and goodly shines the moon!

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: The moon? The sun! It is not moonlight now.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: I say it is the moon that shines so bright.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: I know it is the sun that shines so bright.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Now, by my mother’s son, and that’s myself,
It shall be moon, or star, or what I list,
Or e'er I journey to your father’s house.
[To servants] Go on, and fetch our horses back again.—
Evermore crossed and crossed, nothing but crossed!

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: [To KATHERINE] Say as he says, or we shall never go.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Forward, I pray, since we have come so far,
And be it moon, or sun, or what you please.
An if you please to call it a rush candle,
Henceforth I vow it shall be so for me.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: I say it is the moon.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: I know it is the moon.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Nay, then you lie. It is the blessèd sun.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Then God be blessed, it is the blessèd sun.
But sun it is not, when you say it is not,
And the moon changes even as your mind.
What you will have it named, even that it is,
And so it shall be so for Katherine.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: Petruchio, go thy ways; the field is won.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Well, forward, forward! Thus the bowl should run,
And not unluckily against the bias.
But, soft! Company is coming here.
Enter VINCENTIO

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
[To VINCENTIO]
Good morrow, gentle mistress, where away?—
Tell me, sweet Kate, and tell me truly too,
Hast thou beheld a fresher gentlewoman?
Such war of white and red within her cheeks!
What stars do spangle heaven with such beauty
As those two eyes become that heavenly face?—
Fair lovely maid, once more good day to thee.—
Sweet Kate, embrace her for her beauty’s sake.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
[aside] He will make the man mad, to make a woman of
him.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Young budding virgin, fair and fresh and sweet,
Whither away, or where is thy abode?
Happy the parents of so fair a child.
Happier the man whom favorable stars
Allot thee for his lovely bedfellow!

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Why, how now, Kate! I hope thou art not mad.
This is a man—old, wrinkled, faded, withered—
And not a maiden, as thou say’st he is.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Pardon, old father, my mistaking eyes
That have been so bedazzled with the sun
That everything I look on seemeth green.
Now I perceive thou art a reverend father.
Pardon, I pray thee, for my mad mistaking.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Do, good old grandsire, and withal make known
Which way thou travellest. If along with us,
We shall be joyful of thy company.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
Fair sir, and you, my merry mistress,
That with your strange encounter much amazed me,
My name is called Vincentio, my dwelling Pisa,
And bound I am to Padua, there to visit
A son of mine which long I have not seen.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: What is his name?

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Lucentio, gentle sir.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Happily met, the happier for thy son.
And now by law as well as reverend age,
I may entitle thee my loving father.
The sister to my wife, this gentlewoman,
Thy son by this hath married. Wonder not
Nor be grieved. She is of good esteem,
Her dowry wealthy, and of worthy birth.
Beside, so qualified as may beseem
The spouse of any noble gentleman.
Let me embrace with old Vincentio,
And wander we to see thy honest son,
Who will of thy arrival be full joyous.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
But is this true, or is it else your pleasure,
Like pleasant travelers, to break a jest
Upon the company you overtake?

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: I do assure thee, father, so it is.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Come, go along and see the truth hereof,
For our first merriment hath made thee jealous.
Exeunt all but HORTENSIO

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
Well, Petruchio, this has put me in heart.
Have to my widow, and if she be froward,
Then hast thou taught Hortensio to be untoward.
Exit
Enter BIONDELLO, LUCENTIO, and BIANCA. GREMIO is out before

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: Softly and swiftly, sir, for the priest is ready.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
I fly, Biondello. But they may chance to need thee at
home.
Therefore leave us.
Exit LUCENTIO with BIANCA

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
Nay, faith, I’ll see the church a' your back, and then
come back to my master’s as soon as I can.
Exit

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: I marvel Cambio comes not all this while.
Enter PETRUCHIO, KATHERINE, VINCENTIO and GRUMIO with Attendants

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Sir, here’s the door. This is Lucentio’s house.
My father’s bears more toward the marketplace.
Thither must I, and here I leave you, sir.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
You shall not choose but drink before you go.
I think I shall command your welcome here,
And, by all likelihood, some cheer is toward.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: They’re busy within. You were best knock louder.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
[as VINCENTIO] What’s he that knocks as he would beat
down the gate?

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Is Signior Lucentio within, sir?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
[as VINCENTIO] He’s within, sir, but not to be spoken
withal.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
What if a man bring him a hundred pound or two to make
merry withal?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
[as VINCENTIO] Keep your hundred pounds to yourself. He
shall need none so long as I live.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
[To VINCENTIO] Nay, I told you your son was well
beloved in Padua. —Do you hear, sir? To leave frivolous
circumstances, I pray you tell Signior Lucentio that his
father is come from Pisa and is here at the door to
speak with him.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
[as VINCENTIO] Thou liest. His father is come from
Padua and here looking out at the window.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Art thou his father?

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
[as VINCENTIO] Ay, sir, so his mother says, if I may
believe her.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
[To VINCENTIO] Why, how now, gentleman! Why, this is
flat knavery to take upon you another man’s name.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
[as VINCENTIO] Lay hands on the villain. I believe he
means to cozen somebody in this city under my
countenance.
Enter BIONDELLO

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
[aside] I have seen them in the church together. God
send
'em good shipping! But who is here? Mine old master
Vincentio! Now we are undone and brought to nothing.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: [To BIONDELLO] Come hither, crack-hemp.

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: Hope I may choose, sir.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Come hither, you rogue! What, have you forgot me?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
Forgot you! No, sir. I could not forget you, for I
never saw you before in all my life.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
What, you notorious villain, didst thou never see thy
master’s father, Vincentio?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
What, my old worshipful old master? Yes, marry, sir.
See where he looks out of the window.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Is ’t so, indeed.

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: Help, help, help! Here’s a madman will murder me.
Exit

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text: [as VINCENTIO] Help, son! Help, Signior Baptista!
Exit from above

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Prithee, Kate, let’s stand aside and see the end of
this controversy.
Enter MERCHANT below, TRANIO, BAPTISTA and Servants

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Sir, what are you that offer to beat my
servant?

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
What am I, sir! Nay, what are you, sir? O immortal
gods! O fine villain! A silken doublet, a velvet hose, a
scarlet cloak, and a copatain hat! Oh, I am undone, I
am undone! While I play the good husband at home, my son
and my servant spend all at the university.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: [as LUCENTIO] How now, what’s the matter?

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: What, is the man lunatic?

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Sir, you seem a sober ancient gentleman
by your habit, but your words show you a madman. Why,
sir, what 'cerns it you if I wear pearl and gold? I
thank my good father I am able to maintain it.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Thy father! O villain! He is a sailmaker in Bergamo.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text:
You mistake, sir, you mistake, sir. Pray, what do you
think is his name?

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
His name! As if I knew not his name! I have brought him
up ever since he was three years old, and his name is
Tranio.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text:
[as VINCENTIO] Away, away, mad ass! His name is
Lucentio and he is mine only son, and heir to the lands
of me, Signior Vincentio.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
Lucentio! Oh, he hath murdered his master! Lay hold on
him, I charge you in the Duke’s name. O my son, my son!
Tell me, thou villain, where is my son Lucentio?

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text: [as LUCENTIO] Call forth an officer.
Enter an Officer

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
Carry this mad knave to the jail.—Father Baptista,
I charge you see that he be forthcoming.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Carry me to the jail?

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Stay, officer. He shall not go to prison.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Talk not, Signior Gremio. I say he shall go to prison.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text:
Take heed, Signior Baptista, lest you be cony-catched
in this business. I dare swear this is the right
Vincentio.

units.speaker: MERCHANT
units.text: [as VINCENTIO] Swear, if thou darest.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Nay, I dare not swear it.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
[as LUCENTIO] Then thou wert best say that I am not
Lucentio.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Yes, I know thee to be Signior Lucentio.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Away with the dotard! To the jail with him!

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
Thus strangers may be haled and abused.—O monstrous
villain!
Enter BIONDELLO with LUCENTIO, BIANCA

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
O! We are spoiled and yonder he is! Deny him, forswear
him, or else we are all undone.
Exeunt BIONDELLO, TRANIO and MERCHANT as fast as may be

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: LUCENTIO, and BIANCA kneel

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: Pardon, sweet father.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Lives my sweet son?

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Pardon, dear father.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: How hast thou offended? Where is Lucentio?

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
Here’s Lucentio, right son to the right Vincentio,
That have by marriage made thy daughter mine
While counterfeit supposes bleared thine eyne.

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Here’s packing, with a witness, to deceive us all!

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
Where is that damnèd villain, Tranio,
That faced and braved me in this matter so?

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Why, tell me, is not this my Cambio?

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Cambio is changed into Lucentio.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
Love wrought these miracles. Bianca’s love
Made me exchange my state with Tranio,
While he did bear my countenance in the town,
And happily I have arrivèd at the last
Unto the wishèd haven of my bliss.
What Tranio did, myself enforced him to.
Then pardon him, sweet father, for my sake.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
I’ll slit the villain’s nose that would have sent me to
the jail.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text:
But do you hear, sir, have you married my daughter
without asking my goodwill?

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text:
Fear not, Baptista, we will content you. Go to. But I
will in to be revenged for this villany.
Exit

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: And I, to sound the depth of this knavery.
Exit

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: Look not pale, Bianca. Thy father will not frown.
Exeunt LUCENTIO and BIANCA

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text:
My cake is dough, but I’ll in among the rest,
Out of hope of all but my share of the feast.
Exit

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Husband, let’s follow to see the end of this ado.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: First kiss me, Kate, and we will.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: What, in the midst of the street?

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: What, art thou ashamed of me?

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: No, sir, God forbid, but ashamed to kiss.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Why, then let’s home again. [To GRUMIO] Come, sirrah,
let’s away.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Nay, I will give thee a kiss. [kisses him] Now pray
thee, love, stay.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Is not this well? Come, my sweet Kate.
Better once than never, for never too late.
Exeunt
Enter BAPTISTA, VINCENTIO, GREMIO, the MERCHANT, LUCENTIO, BIANCA, PETRUCHIO, KATHERINE, HORTENSIO, WIDOW, TRANIO, BIONDELLO, and GRUMIO, with the Servingmen bringing in a banquet

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
At last, though long, our jarring notes agree,
And time it is when raging war is done
To smile at ’scapes and perils overblown.
My fair Bianca, bid my father welcome,
While I with selfsame kindness welcome thine.
Brother Petruchio, sister Katherina,
And thou, Hortensio, with thy loving widow,
Feast with the best, and welcome to my house.
My banquet is to close our stomachs up,
After our great good cheer. Pray you, sit down,
For now we sit to chat as well as eat.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Nothing but sit and sit, and eat and eat!

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Padua affords this kindness, son Petruchio.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Padua affords nothing but what is kind.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: For both our sakes, I would that word were true.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Now, for my life, Hortensio fears his widow.

units.speaker: WIDOW
units.text: Then never trust me if I be afeard.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
You are very sensible, and yet you miss my sense:
I mean, Hortensio is afeard of you.

units.speaker: WIDOW
units.text: He that is giddy thinks the world turns round.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Roundly replied.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: Mistress, how mean you that?

units.speaker: WIDOW
units.text: Thus I conceive by him.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Conceives by me? How likes Hortensio that?

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: My widow says, thus she conceives her tale.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Very well mended. Kiss him for that, good widow.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
“He that is giddy thinks the world turns round”—
I pray you, tell me what you meant by that.

units.speaker: WIDOW
units.text:
Your husband being troubled with a shrew
Measures my husband’s sorrow by his woe.
And now you know my meaning.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: A very mean meaning.

units.speaker: WIDOW
units.text: Right, I mean you.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: And I am mean indeed, respecting you.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: To her, Kate!

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: To her, widow!

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: A hundred marks, my Kate does put her down.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: That’s my office.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Spoke like an officer! Ha' to thee, lad!

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: How likes Gremio these quick-witted folks?

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text: Believe me, sir, they butt together well.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
Head and butt! An hasty-witted body
Would say your head and butt were head and horn.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: Ay, mistress bride, hath that awakened you?

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Ay, but not frighted me. Therefore I’ll sleep again.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Nay, that you shall not. Since you have begun,
Have at you for a bitter jest or two!

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
Am I your bird? I mean to shift my bush,
And then pursue me as you draw your bow.—
You are welcome all.
Exeunt BIANCA, KATHERINE and WIDOW

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
She hath prevented me. Here, Signior Tranio,
This bird you aimed at, though you hit her not.—
Therefore a health to all that shot and missed.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
Oh, sir, Lucentio slipped me like his greyhound,
Which runs himself and catches for his master.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: A good swift simile, but something currish.

units.speaker: TRANIO
units.text:
'Tis well, sir, that you hunted for yourself.
'Tis thought your deer does hold you at a bay.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Oh, Oh, Petruchio! Tranio hits you now.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: I thank thee for that gird, good Tranio.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: Confess, confess, hath he not hit you here?

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
He has a little galled me, I confess.
And, as the jest did glance away from me,
'Tis ten to one it maimed you two outright.

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text:
Now, in good sadness, son Petruchio,
I think thou hast the veriest shrew of all.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Well, I say no. And therefore, for assurance,
Let’s each one send unto his wife;
And he whose wife is most obedient
To come at first when he doth send for her,
Shall win the wager which we will propose.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: Content. What’s the wager?

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: Twenty crowns.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Twenty crowns?
I’ll venture so much of my hawk or hound,
But twenty times so much upon my wife.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: A hundred then.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: Content.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: A match! 'Tis done.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: Who shall begin?

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
That will I.
Go, Biondello, bid your mistress come to me.

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text: I go.
Exit

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Son, I’ll be your half Bianca comes.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: I’ll have no halves. I’ll bear it all myself.
Enter BIONDELLO

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: How now, what news?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
Sir, my mistress sends you word
That she is busy, and she cannot come.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
How! “She’s busy, and she cannot come!”
Is that an answer?

units.speaker: GREMIO
units.text:
Ay, and a kind one too.
Pray God, sir, your wife send you not a worse.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: I hope better.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
Sirrah Biondello, go and entreat my wife
To come to me forthwith.
Exit BIONDELLO

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
O, ho, entreat her!
Nay, then she must needs come.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text:
I am afraid, sir,
Do what you can, yours will not be entreated.
Enter BIONDELLO

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: Now, where’s my wife?

units.speaker: BIONDELLO
units.text:
She says you have some goodly jest in hand.
She will not come. She bids you come to her.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Worse and worse. She will not come!
O vile, intolerable, not to be endured!—
Sirrah Grumio, go to your mistress,
Say I command her to come to me.
Exit GRUMIO

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: I know her answer.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: What?

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: She will not.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: The fouler fortune mine, and there an end.
Enter KATHERINE

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text: Now, by my holidam, here comes Katherina!

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: What is your will, sir, that you send for me?

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Where is your sister, and Hortensio’s wife?

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text: They sit conferring by the parlor fire.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Go fetch them hither. If they deny to come,
Swinge me them soundly forth unto their husbands.
Away, I say, and bring them hither straight.
Exit KATHERINE

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: Here is a wonder, if you talk of a wonder.

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: And so it is. I wonder what it bodes.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Marry, peace it bodes, and love, and quiet life,
And awful rule, and right supremacy,
And, to be short, what not that’s sweet and happy?

units.speaker: BAPTISTA
units.text:
Now, fair befall thee, good Petruchio!
The wager thou hast won, and I will add
Unto their losses twenty thousand crowns,
Another dowry to another daughter,
For she is changed as she had never been.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Nay, I will win my wager better yet,
And show more sign of her obedience,
Her new-built virtue and obedience.
Enter KATHERINE with BIANCA and WIDOW

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
See where she comes and brings your froward wives
As prisoners to her womanly persuasion.—
Katherine, that cap of yours becomes you not.
Off with that bauble, throw it underfoot.

units.speaker: WIDOW
units.text:
Lord, let me never have a cause to sigh,
Till I be brought to such a silly pass!

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Fie! What a foolish duty call you this?

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text:
I would your duty were as foolish too.
The wisdom of your duty, fair Bianca,
Hath cost me an hundred crowns since suppertime.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: The more fool you for laying on my duty.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
Katherine, I charge thee, tell these headstrong women
What duty they do owe their lords and husbands.

units.speaker: WIDOW
units.text: Come, come, you’re mocking. We will have no telling.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Come on, I say, and first begin with her.

units.speaker: WIDOW
units.text: She shall not.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: I say she shall.—And first begin with her.

units.speaker: KATHERINE
units.text:
Fie, fie! Unknit that threat'ning unkind brow
And dart not scornful glances from those eyes
To wound thy lord, thy king, thy governor.
It blots thy beauty as frosts do bite the meads,
Confounds thy fame as whirlwinds shake fair buds,
And in no sense is meet or amiable.
A woman moved is like a fountain troubled,
Muddy, ill-seeming, thick, bereft of beauty,
And while it is so, none so dry or thirsty
Will deign to sip or touch one drop of it.
Thy husband is thy lord, thy life, thy keeper,
Thy head, thy sovereign, one that cares for thee,
And for thy maintenance commits his body
To painful labor both by sea and land,
To watch the night in storms, the day in cold,
Whilst thou liest warm at home, secure and safe,
And craves no other tribute at thy hands
But love, fair looks and true obedience—
Too little payment for so great a debt.
Such duty as the subject owes the prince,
Even such a woman oweth to her husband.
And when she is froward, peevish, sullen, sour,
And not obedient to his honest will,
What is she but a foul contending rebel
And graceless traitor to her loving lord?
I am ashamed that women are so simple
To offer war where they should kneel for peace;
Or seek for rule, supremacy and sway
When they are bound to serve, love, and obey.
Why are our bodies soft and weak and smooth,
Unapt to toil and trouble in the world,
But that our soft conditions and our hearts
Should well agree with our external parts?
Come, come, you froward and unable worms!
My mind hath been as big as one of yours,
My heart as great, my reason haply more,
To bandy word for word and frown for frown.
But now I see our lances are but straws,
Our strength as weak, our weakness past compare,
That seeming to be most which we indeed least are.
Then vail your stomachs, for it is no boot,
And place your hands below your husband’s foot:
In token of which duty, if he please,
My hand is ready, may it do him ease.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text: Why, there’s a wench! Come on and kiss me, Kate.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: Well, go thy ways, old lad, for thou shalt ha ’t.

units.speaker: VINCENTIO
units.text: 'Tis a good hearing when children are toward.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: But a harsh hearing when women are froward.

units.speaker: PETRUCHIO
units.text:
[To LUCENTIO]
Come, Kate, we’ll to bed.
We three are married, but you two are sped.
'Twas I won the wager, though you hit the white,
And, being a winner, God give you good night!
Exeunt PETRUCHIO and KATHERINE

units.speaker: HORTENSIO
units.text: Now, go thy ways, thou hast tamed a curst shrew.

units.speaker: LUCENTIO
units.text: 'Tis a wonder, by your leave, she will be tamed so.
Exeunt

sentences.text: Enter SLY and HOSTESS

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text: I’ll pheeze you, in faith.

sentences.speaker: HOSTESS
sentences.text: A pair of stocks, you rogue!

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
Y'are a baggage, the Slys are no rogues. Look in the
chronicles—we came in with Richard Conqueror. Therefore
paucas pallabris : let the world slide. Sessa!

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:

chronicles—we came in with Richard Conqueror. Therefore
paucas pallabris : let the world slide. Sessa!

sentences.speaker: HOSTESS
sentences.text: You will not pay for the glasses you have burst?

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
No, not a denier. Go by, Saint Jeronimy. Go to thy cold
bed and warm thee.

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
No, not a denier. Go by, Saint Jeronimy. Go to thy cold
bed and warm thee.

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
No, not a denier. Go by, Saint Jeronimy. Go to thy cold
bed and warm thee.

sentences.speaker: HOSTESS
sentences.text: I know my remedy. I must go fetch the thirdborough.

sentences.speaker: HOSTESS
sentences.text: I know my remedy. I must go fetch the thirdborough.
Exit

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
Third, or fourth, or fifth borough, I’ll answer him by
law.
I’ll not budge an inch, boy. Let him come, and kindly.

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
Third, or fourth, or fifth borough, I’ll answer him by
law.
I’ll not budge an inch, boy. Let him come, and kindly.

sentences.text:
Third, or fourth, or fifth borough, I’ll answer him by
law.
I’ll not budge an inch, boy. Let him come, and kindly.

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
Third, or fourth, or fifth borough, I’ll answer him by
law.
I’ll not budge an inch, boy. Let him come, and kindly.
Falls asleep
Wind horns Enter a LORD from hunting, with his train
Wind horns Enter a LORD from hunting, with his train

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Huntsman, I charge thee, tender well my hounds.
Breathe Merriman, the poor cur is embossed,
And couple Clowder with the deep-mouthed brach.
Saw’st thou not, boy, how Silver made it good
At the hedge corner, in the coldest fault?
I would not lose the dog for twenty pound.

sentences.speaker: FIRST HUNTSMAN
sentences.text:
Why, Belman is as good as he, my lord.
He cried upon it at the merest loss,
And twice today picked out the dullest scent.
Trust me, I take him for the better dog.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Thou art a fool. If Echo were as fleet,
I would esteem him worth a dozen such.
But sup them well and look unto them all.
Tomorrow I intend to hunt again.

sentences.speaker: FIRST HUNTSMAN
sentences.text: I will, my lord.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: What’s here? One dead, or drunk? See, doth he breathe?

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: What’s here? One dead, or drunk? See, doth he breathe?

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: What’s here? One dead, or drunk? See, doth he breathe?

sentences.speaker: SECOND HUNTSMAN
sentences.text:
He breathes, my lord. Were he not warmed with ale,
This were a bed but cold to sleep so soundly.

sentences.speaker: SECOND HUNTSMAN
sentences.text:
He breathes, my lord. Were he not warmed with ale,
This were a bed but cold to sleep so soundly.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
O monstrous beast, how like a swine he lies!
Grim death, how foul and loathsome is thine image!
Sirs, I will practice on this drunken man.
What think you: if he were conveyed to bed,
Wrapped in sweet clothes, rings put upon his fingers,
A most delicious banquet by his bed,
And brave attendants near him when he wakes,
Would not the beggar then forget himself?

O monstrous beast, how like a swine he lies!

Sirs, I will practice on this drunken man.
What think you: if he were conveyed to bed,
Wrapped in sweet clothes, rings put upon his fingers,
A most delicious banquet by his bed,
And brave attendants near him when he wakes,
Would not the beggar then forget himself?

sentences.speaker: FIRST HUNTSMAN
sentences.text: Believe me, lord, I think he cannot choose.

sentences.speaker: SECOND HUNTSMAN
sentences.text: It would seem strange unto him when he waked.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Even as a flatt'ring dream or worthless fancy.
Then take him up and manage well the jest.
Carry him gently to my fairest chamber
And hang it round with all my wanton pictures.
Balm his foul head in warm distilled waters
And burn sweet wood to make the lodging sweet.
Procure me music ready when he wakes,
To make a dulcet and a heavenly sound.
And if he chance to speak, be ready straight
And with a low submissive reverence
Say, “What is it your Honor will command?”
Let one attend him with a silver basin
Full of rose-water and bestrewed with flowers,
Another bear the ewer, the third a diaper,
And say, “Will ’t please your Lordship cool your
hands?”
Someone be ready with a costly suit
And ask him what apparel he will wear.
Another tell him of his hounds and horse,
And that his lady mourns at his disease.
Persuade him that he hath been lunatic,
And when he says he is, say that he dreams,
For he is nothing but a mighty lord.
This do, and do it kindly, gentle sirs.
It will be pastime passing excellent
If it be husbanded with modesty.

sentences.speaker: FIRST HUNTSMAN
sentences.text:
My lord, I warrant you we will play our part
As he shall think by our true diligence
He is no less than what we say he is.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Take him up gently, and to bed with him,
And each one to his office when he wakes.
Some servants carry out SLY. Sound trumpets
Some servants carry out SLY. Sound trumpets

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: Sirrah, go see what trumpet ’tis that sounds.
Exit Servingman

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Belike some noble gentleman that means,
Traveling some journey, to repose him here.
Enter SERVANT

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: How now! who is it?

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: How now! who is it?

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text:
An’t please your Honor, players
That offer service to your Lordship.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: Bid them come near.
Enter PLAYERS

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: Now, fellows, you are welcome.

sentences.speaker: PLAYERS
sentences.text: We thank your Honor.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text: Do you intend to stay with me tonight?

sentences.speaker: A PLAYER
sentences.text: So please your Lordship to accept our duty.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
With all my heart. This fellow I remember
Since once he played a farmer’s eldest son.
'Twas where you wooed the gentlewoman so well.
I have forgot your name, but sure that part
Was aptly fitted and naturally performed.

sentences.speaker: A PLAYER
sentences.text: I think ’twas Soto that your Honor means.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
'Tis very true. Thou didst it excellent.
Well, you are come to me in happy time,
The rather for I have some sport in hand
Wherein your cunning can assist me much.
There is a lord will hear you play tonight;
But I am doubtful of your modesties,
Lest over-eyeing of his odd behavior—
For yet his Honor never heard a play—
You break into some merry passion
And so offend him. For I tell you, sirs,
If you should smile, he grows impatient.

sentences.speaker: A PLAYER
sentences.text:
Fear not, my lord, we can contain ourselves
Were he the veriest antic in the world.

sentences.speaker: A PLAYER
sentences.text:
Fear not, my lord, we can contain ourselves
Were he the veriest antic in the world.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Go, sirrah, take them to the buttery
And give them friendly welcome every one.
Let them want nothing that my house affords.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Go, sirrah, take them to the buttery
And give them friendly welcome every one.
Let them want nothing that my house affords.
Exit one with the PLAYERS

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Sirrah, go you to Barthol’mew, my page,
And see him dressed in all suits like a lady.
That done, conduct him to the drunkard’s chamber
And call him “madam,” do him obeisance.
Tell him from me, as he will win my love,
He bear himself with honorable action,
Such as he hath observed in noble ladies
Unto their lords, by them accomplishèd.
Such duty to the drunkard let him do
With soft low tongue and lowly courtesy,
And say, “What is ’t your Honor will command,
Wherein your lady and your humble wife
May show her duty and make known her love?”
And then with kind embracements, tempting kisses,
And with declining head into his bosom,
Bid him shed tears, as being overjoyed
To see her noble lord restored to health,
Who for this seven years hath esteemed him
No better than a poor and loathsome beggar.
And if the boy have not a woman’s gift
To rain a shower of commanded tears,
An onion will do well for such a shift,
Which in a napkin being close conveyed
Shall in despite enforce a watery eye.
See this dispatched with all the haste thou canst:
Anon I’ll give thee more instructions.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
I know the boy will well usurp the grace,
Voice, gait, and action of a gentlewoman.
I long to hear him call the drunkard “husband,”
And how my men will stay themselves from laughter
When they do homage to this simple peasant.
I’ll in to counsel them. Haply my presence
May well abate the over-merry spleen
Which otherwise would grow into extremes.

Enter aloft SLY, the drunkard, with Attendants, some with apparel, others with basin and ewer and other appurtenances, and LORD dressed as an attendant.

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text: For God’s sake, a pot of small ale.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text: Will ’t please your Lordship drink a cup of sack?

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text: Will ’t please your Honor taste of these conserves?

sentences.speaker: THIRD SERVANT
sentences.text: What raiment will your Honor wear today?

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
I am Christophero Sly. Call not me “Honor” nor
“Lordship.” I ne'er drank sack in my life. An if you
give me any conserves, give me conserves of beef. Ne'er
ask me what raiment I’ll wear, for I have no more
doublets than backs, no more stockings than legs, nor no
more shoes than feet, nay sometime more feet than
shoes, or such shoes as my toes look through the
over-leather.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Heaven cease this idle humor in your Honor!
Oh, that a mighty man of such descent,
Of such possessions and so high esteem,
Should be infusèd with so foul a spirit!

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Heaven cease this idle humor in your Honor!
Oh, that a mighty man of such descent,
Of such possessions and so high esteem,
Should be infusèd with so foul a spirit!

sentences.speaker: SLY
sentences.text:
What, would you make me mad? Am not I Christopher Sly,
old Sly’s son of Burton Heath, by birth a peddler, by
education a cardmaker, by transmutation a bearherd, and
now by present profession a tinker? Ask Marian Hacket,
the fat alewife of Wincot, if she know me not! If she
say I am not fourteen pence on the score for sheer ale,
score me up for the lying’st knave in Christendom. What!
I am not bestraught! Here’s—

sentences.speaker: THIRD SERVANT
sentences.text: O, this it is that makes your lady mourn!

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text: O, this is it that makes your servants droop!

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
Hence comes it that your kindred shuns your house,
As beaten hence by your strange lunacy.
O noble lord, bethink thee of thy birth,
Call home thy ancient thoughts from banishment,
And banish hence these abject lowly dreams.
Look how thy servants do attend on thee,
Each in his office ready at thy beck.
Wilt thou have music? Hark! Apollo plays,

Music

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
And twenty cagèd nightingales do sing:
Or wilt thou sleep? We’ll have thee to a couch
Softer and sweeter than the lustful bed
On purpose trimmed up for Semiramis.
Say thou wilt walk, we will bestrew the ground.
Or wilt thou ride? Thy horses shall be trapped,
Their harness studded all with gold and pearl.
Dost thou love hawking? Thou hast hawks will soar
Above the morning lark. Or wilt thou hunt?
Thy hounds shall make the welkin answer them
And fetch shrill echoes from the hollow earth.

Thy hounds shall make the welkin answer them
And fetch shrill echoes from the hollow earth.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
And twenty cagèd nightingales do sing:
Or wilt thou sleep? We’ll have thee to a couch
Softer and sweeter than the lustful bed
On purpose trimmed up for Semiramis.
Say thou wilt walk, we will bestrew the ground.
Or wilt thou ride? Thy horses shall be trapped,
Their harness studded all with gold and pearl.
Dost thou love hawking? Thou hast hawks will soar
Above the morning lark. Or wilt thou hunt?
Thy hounds shall make the welkin answer them
And fetch shrill echoes from the hollow earth.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
Say thou wilt course. Thy greyhounds are as swift
As breathed stags, ay, fleeter than the roe.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
Say thou wilt course. Thy greyhounds are as swift
As breathed stags, ay, fleeter than the roe.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text:
Dost thou love pictures? We will fetch thee straight
Adonis painted by a running brook
And Cytherea all in sedges hid,
Which seem to move and wanton with her breath,
Even as the waving sedges play with wind.
